g.data-narration: Welcome to this lecture on Challenges of Artificial Neural Networks. We'll explore key limitations affecting ANN performance.
ANNs are powerful but face significant challenges including data quality issues, overfitting, underfitting, computational complexity, and interpretability.
g.text: Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) are widely used in image recognition,
NLP, and decision-making. However, several challenges limit their
performance and adoption in real-world applications.

g.data-narration: Understanding these challenges is crucial for building robust, efficient, and trustworthy AI systems that generalize well.
g.text: → Data quality issues lead to biased models and poor generalization
→ Overfitting/underfitting affect model reliability on unseen data
→ Interpretability builds trust in critical AI applications

g.data-narration: Let's examine data quality issues - the foundation of any ANN. Poor data leads to poor model performance regardless of architecture.
g.text: ANNs require large amounts of high-quality data to learn and generalize.
Real-world data often has issues: noisy data, missing values, and imbalanced data.
Three main problems affect data quality

g.data-narration: Noisy data contains errors or irrelevant information. It leads to poor model performance and increases the risk of overfitting.
g.text: Definition: Data containing errors, outliers, or irrelevant information

g.data-narration: Missing values occur due to sensor failures or data entry errors. They create biased models and reduce overall prediction accuracy.
g.text: Definition: Absent data points due to sensor failures, data entry errors, or privacy issues

g.data-narration: Imbalanced data has unequal class distribution, causing models to favor the majority class and ignore minority predictions.
g.text: Definition: Unequal distribution of classes in the dataset

g.data-narration: Now let's explore overfitting and underfitting - critical training challenges that determine model generalization ability.
g.text: Two common problems that affect model generalization performance.
Finding the right balance is key to building effective ANNs.

g.data-narration: Overfitting occurs when a model is too complex and fits training data too closely, failing to generalize to new data.
g.text: Definition: Model is too complex and fits training data too closely

g.data-narration: Underfitting happens when a model is too simple to capture patterns in data, resulting in poor performance on both train and test data.
g.text: Definition: Model is too simple and fails to capture underlying patterns

g.data-narration: Let's examine computational complexity - a major practical challenge when training and deploying large-scale neural networks.
g.text: ANNs can be computationally expensive to train, especially for large datasets.
Complexity depends on model size, data size, and algorithm choice.

g.data-narration: Three key factors affect computational complexity: model size (layers and neurons), data size (samples and features), and algorithm choice.
Finally, let's explore interpretability - a critical challenge when AI systems must explain their decisions to humans.
g.text: ANNs are often criticized for their "black box" nature - difficult to understand
how they arrive at predictions. This limits trust in critical applications.

g.data-narration: Interpretability means understanding how models make decisions. It's crucial for healthcare, finance, and legal applications requiring accountability.
g.text: Definition: Ability to understand how a model makes predictions

g.data-narration: In summary, ANN challenges include data quality issues, overfitting, underfitting, computational complexity, and interpretability. Understanding these is key to building effective AI.
g.text: Mastering these challenges is essential for building robust, trustworthy AI systems
